_comment: Copy to config.json and edit. EVERYTHING is configuration — the agent hardcodes nothing. Secrets are best kept in *_file paths (re-read on every use → rotate without restart); never commit config.json or the secrets/ folder (both are .gitignored).
_bind: The interface THIS agent listens on. Keep it private: loopback if the MCP router runs on the same host, or a private/WireGuard IP (e.g. 10.x.y.z) so a remote router host can reach it. NEVER a public interface — and firewall the port to that network anyway.
_idle_timeout_s: Seconds without any /view call before a vault's tunnel auto-closes. Generous values keep tunnels warm across a conversation (a warm tunnel answers <1s; a cold start takes ~15-18s).
_token_file: Optional shared secret (2nd lock after the network). When this file exists, /view requires a matching X-View-Token header — set the SAME value as OBSIDIAN_ROUTER_VIEW_AGENT_TOKEN on the router instance. Generate one with: openssl rand -hex 24 > view-agent.token. Absent file = gate disabled.

alice._gui_url: Local URL of this vault's Obsidian GUI (e.g. a linuxserver/obsidian Selkies container published on loopback). This is what the tunnel exposes.
alice._gui: Basic-auth credentials of the GUI — baked into the returned URL so the user types nothing. Omit both if the GUI has no auth (the raw tunnel URL is returned). Inline `gui_password` is also accepted but a *_file keeps the config secret-free.
alice._open: Optional: base URL of the vault's Local REST API + its key, used to navigate Obsidian onto the requested note (POST {open_url}/open/<note>, served by the mcp-router-bridge plugin >= 0.2.0) before returning the link. Omit to skip navigation — the link still works, just lands on the last-open note.
